extraction.one_line_summary: LEKNESSUND Andreas took pink jersey finishing 2nd to BARGUIL Warren from 7-rider break that survived by 2+ minutes after 80km of attacks to form.
tactical_patterns: UAE Emirates let pink jersey Remco slip away willingly; no chase suggests they preferred avoiding early leadership burden.
80km required for break formation shows peloton aggressively controlled early dangerous moves despite hard parcours.
Leknessund sacrificed stage win for GC positioning by fetching bonus seconds at final intermediate sprint.

rider_signals.LEKNESSUND Andreas: positive: 23-year-old Arctic Circle rider showed tactical maturity prioritizing bonus seconds and GC over stage win in 7-man break
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: willingly surrendered pink after 3 days, suggesting team planned short early tenure to avoid defensive duties
rider_signals.MAEDA Tadej: negative: UAE leader caught in early split and required tow back despite team having pink jersey
